readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Hidden Work of Sleep
paragraphs: For a long time, sleep was treated as little more than wasted time, an empty pause between the busy hours of waking life. Yet research over the past few decades has shown the opposite. While the body lies still, the brain remains remarkably active, carrying out tasks that simply cannot be done while we are awake.

One of these tasks is sorting through memories. During the night, the brain replays parts of the day, strengthening the connections that matter and quietly fading those that do not. This is why students who sleep well after studying often remember far more than those who stay up late trying to force the facts into their heads.

Sleep also acts as a kind of cleaning service. As we rest, the brain flushes away waste products that build up during the day. When we miss this nightly clean, the effects soon show: our mood darkens, our attention wanders, and even simple decisions begin to feel strangely difficult.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Far from being wasted time, sleep is an active period in which the brain sorts memories and clears away waste.
Sleep is simply an empty pause that has no real effect on how the brain works.
People who sleep less than others always remember information much better.
The brain completely shuts down and does nothing at all while we are asleep.

questions.before: Students who sleep well after studying tend to remember more because the brain
questions.after: the important connections during the night.

questions.prompt: 睡眠が脳の「掃除」の役割を果たすことを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the brain replays parts of the day
the brain flushes away waste products that build up during the day
our mood darkens, our attention wanders

questions.sentence: In other words, a good night's rest can do more for learning than an extra hour of late-night study.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で述べられている内容として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: During sleep the brain removes waste, and missing this rest soon harms mood, attention, and decisions.
Sleep adds waste to the brain, which is why we feel refreshed in the morning.
Missing sleep has no effect on mood or on the ability to make simple decisions.
The brain cleans itself only while we are awake and busy during the day.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Tiny Workers of the Garden
paragraphs: When we picture a bee, we often think only of its sting or its honey. Yet the real importance of bees lies in something far quieter. As they fly from flower to flower in search of food, they carry tiny grains of pollen on their bodies, helping plants to make seeds and fruit.

This service, known as pollination, is worth far more than the honey bees produce. A large share of the fruits and vegetables we eat depends on insects to spread their pollen. Without these visits, many crops would yield little, and our meals would become much smaller and far less colourful.

In recent years, however, bee numbers have fallen in many parts of the world. Disease, the loss of wild flowers, and the heavy use of chemicals on farms have all played a part. Some cities have begun to respond by planting flowers along roads and on rooftops, giving these small but vital workers a place to feed once more.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Bees matter most because they pollinate the crops we eat, yet their numbers are falling and need our help.
Bees are important only because of the honey and the sting that they give us.
The number of bees in the world has grown quickly and there is nothing to worry about.
Cities have decided that planting flowers does nothing to help bees survive.

questions.before: Bees help plants make seeds and fruit by carrying tiny grains of

questions.prompt: ハチの数が減っている原因を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: they carry tiny grains of pollen on their bodies
this service, known as pollination, is worth far more than the honey
Disease, the loss of wild flowers, and the heavy use of chemicals on farms
planting flowers along roads and on rooftops

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Bees quietly support our food supply through pollination, but they are in decline and cities are starting to help.
Bees are mainly a danger to people and should be removed from gardens and farms.
Honey is the only reason that bees are useful to humans in any way.
Bee numbers are rising everywhere, so no action is needed to protect them.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind sending me the updated schedule before you leave the office today?
The train leaves at a quarter to seven, so we should arrive by six forty.
Had I known about the storm, I would never have planned the trip for this weekend.
He kept checking his phone as though he were waiting for important news.
The fresh bread crackled softly as she sliced through its golden crust.
I'm not sure whether we should wait for them or start the meeting without them.
